headlines.title: Saudis Turn to Russian Fuel Oil as Iran War Saps Fossil Power Supplies
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia continues to snap up high volumes of Russia’s fuel oil as the Hormuz crisis has forced shut oil and gas wells and reduced domestic supply for power generation amid rising temperatures. headlines.title: U.S. Airlines Set To Pocket $40 Billion As Jet Fuel Prices Crash
headlines.summary: U.S. airlines stand to save billions in dollars on jet fuel costs after the US-Iran peace deal sent oil prices sharply lower. headlines.title: When Will Gasoline Prices Return to Pre-War Levels?
headlines.summary: Gasoline prices have started to fall, and that is welcome news for drivers. headlines.title: Iran’s Oil Exports Through Hormuz Hit Wartime High
headlines.summary: Iran isn’t wasting any time moving its oil out of the Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz after the U.S. lifted the naval blockade outside the chokepoint and the U.S. and Iran discuss a framework on a lasting peace deal. headlines.title: Kuwait Offers Gulf Oil Loadings as Producers Seek Hormuz Outlet
headlines.summary: Kuwait is offering naphtha for loading at its ports deep into the Persian Gulf in the first such tender in months, as Middle Eastern oil producers seek to raise shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.
